response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable full-stack and systems engineer with genuine strengths in distributed systems and testing culture, but he is fundamentally misaligned with the core requirements of this role. The position demands 8 years of C++ experience; the candidate's C++ exposure amounts to roughly 6 months at National Instruments and a bachelor's thesis project, with the rest of his career built on TypeScript, DLang, Node.js, and web technologies. There is also no evidence of production-level cloud architecture ownership, formal technical leadership over cloud or DevOps teams, or the infrastructure depth (compute, storage, hardware architecture) this role requires at a mid-senior leadership level.
strengths: Strong testing and software craftsmanship culture — TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development practiced and taught across multiple roles
Distributed systems exposure at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic, handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency, async I/O design
Demonstrated knowledge-sharing and informal mentorship through workshops, conference talks, and organizing recurring code kata sessions
concerns: C++ experience is critically insufficient — the role requires 8 years minimum; candidate has approximately 6 months of C/C++ work and one academic project
No production cloud architecture ownership — AWS CDK usage at Optilyz and Kubernetes for a personal project do not constitute the large-scale cloud infrastructure leadership the role demands
No formal technical leadership history managing priorities, roadmaps, or mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers — staff engineer titles without documented lead responsibilities over teams or platforms
